channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: ‘They were laughing’: Israel’s use of rape and sexual abuse in prisons
item.description: Former detainees detail systematic torture and sexual violence, including rape, while in Israeli custody.

item.title: Trump says Iran deal in ‘final throes’
item.description: Donald Trump said that he's in the 'final throes' of a deal with Iran.

item.title: Spain cruise past Peru in final World Cup 2026 warm-up match
item.description: Mikel Oyarzabal opens the scoring after two minutes as Spain comfortably win their final World Cup warm-up in Puebla.

item.title: China’s Xi, North Korea’s Kim pledge to boost ties at rare Pyongyang summit
item.description: President Xi Jinping is on a rare visit to North Korea, where he commits to deeper cooperation with Kim Jong Un.

item.title: Iran war day 102: Trump warns Israel against new strikes as ceasefire holds
item.description: Iran warns fighting could restart as tensions remain high and Trump urges Israel to protect the fragile ceasefire.

item.title: Wembanyama scores 32 as Spurs power past Knicks in Game 3 of NBA Finals
item.description: San Antonio Spurs net first win in Game 3 of best-of-seven NBA Finals with crucial 115-111 victory at New York Knicks.

item.title: Palestine weekly wrap: Israel presses deeper into Gaza as Cairo talks begin
item.description: Israel pushes deeper into Gaza, killing Palestinians across the enclave.

item.title: Iran’s lakes are vanishing: Satellite images show a deepening water crisis
item.description: Years of drought, falling rainfall and unsustainable water use have been worsened further by the US-Israel war.

item.title: Race to find survivors as Philippines quake toll rises to 37
item.description: The quake inflicted some of the worst damage on General Santos, a city of about 720,000 people in southern Mindanao.

item.title: Nuclear weapons spending surges to record high of $119bn, report says
item.description: International Campaign to Abolish Nuclear Weapons says states spent an extra $16.8bn on their nuclear arsenals in 2025.

item.title: ‘As close as lips and teeth’: The highs and lows of China-North Korea ties
item.description: Chinese President Xi has been welcomed in Pyongyang for his first visit since 2019.

item.title: Two years after Israel’s Nuseirat ‘rescue’ success, we are still bleeding
item.description: My family survived the massacre that freed four captives. Its wounds and nightmares have not left us.

item.title: India’s fertility rate falls below replacement level: Why it matters
item.description: India's fertility rate falls to 1.9 children per woman, with consequences for the workforce, the elderly and economy.

item.title: Ebola death toll in DR Congo climbs to 101
item.description: The Democratic Republic of the Congo is grappling with its 17th Ebola outbreak since virus was first identified in 1976.

item.title: FIFA drops Somali World Cup referee after US denies him entry
item.description: Award-winning Somali referee, Omar Artan, has been removed from the World Cup roster after being denied entry into US

item.title: Israel kills 14 in southern Lebanon after trading fire with Iran
item.description: Killings come as Iran warned of 'crushing blows' if Israeli attacks continued in Lebanon, including in the south.

item.title: Marwan Barghouti’s son calls for his release on stage at Berlin concert
item.description: At Berlin’s Citadel Music Festival, activist Arab Barghouti called for the release of his father.

item.title: Israel intensifies strikes in southern Lebanon killing 12 people
item.description: Al Jazeera’s Obaida Hitto reports on the strikes he witnessed in and around Tyre.

item.title: Trump says US in ‘final throes’ of Iran deal, Hormuz strait to open in days
item.description: The president warned Netanyahu he would be on his own if attacks continue after Israel and Iran paused fighting

item.title: US lists China’s BYD, Alibaba, Baidu as ‘Chinese military companies’
item.description: Chinese embassy in Washington, DC, condemns designation, calling it 'discriminatory'.

item.title: Botswana diamond slump hits miners living on the edge of survival
item.description: Botswana’s diamond-driven economy is under mounting strain as global demand weakens and production falls.

item.title: New York City crowd boos US President Donald Trump at NBA playoff game
item.description: US President Donald Trump was loudly booed when he was shown on screens before Game 3 of the NBA Finals.

item.title: UN questions legality of Israeli forced evacuation orders in Lebanon
item.description: A UN spokesman says forced evacuation orders issued across southern and eastern Lebanon are nearly impossible to follow

item.title: ICC prosecutor suspended pending vote on sexual misconduct claims
item.description: Karim Khan rejects suspension as ICC governing body refers him to disciplinary proceedings before member states.

item.title: Israel continues strikes on Lebanon despite halting attacks on Iran
item.description: Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu acknowledged a halt in fighting with Iran but will continue operations against Hezbollah.
